title: Crunchy Briny Canadian Fish Chowder
description: A hearty, one-pot Canadian dish featuring crunchy croutons, a briny broth, and a hint of peanut butter. This pescatarian-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner or a cozy night in.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion, celery, and carrot, and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the haddock and cook for 2-3 minutes, until lightly browned.
Pour in the fish stock and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
Stir in the heavy cream, peanut butter, salt, and pepper until the peanut butter is fully incorporated.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 1-2 minutes, until heated through.
Stir in the crushed potato chips and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the chips are crispy and golden brown.
Serve the chowder hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
Cover the pot and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
